codeReview.summary: All five polish items are implemented correctly. (1) TTL/max-size eviction in gas-price.ts prevents unbounded cache growth with clean TTL checks on reads and a post-write eviction sweep. (2) Logger usage replaces silent catch blocks with logger.debug calls including chainId and error context. (3) ArrowDown-when-closed behavior now exactly mirrors the focus handler (sets previousChainId, pinnedChainId, clears input). (4) Curve test routing now uses distinct mock instances per createCurve() call via an instance queue, with chain-specific output assertions proving correct routing. (5) Autocomplete regression test verifies refresh() calls getCurrentChainId() and does not read chainIdInput.value directly. Code is clean, well-documented, and all 162 tests pass.

issues.description: Typo in test name: 'preferves more recent entries during eviction' should be 'preserves'. Cosmetic only, does not affect test correctness.

evictStaleEntries() is only called after writing a new cache entry (line 178). If no new entries are written for a long time, expired entries remain in the Map (though they are individually rejected on read via TTL check). This is acceptable since stale entries don't cause incorrect behavior, but a periodic cleanup could be considered for long-running servers with many chains.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fullstack-worker skill procedure (step 4) requires interactive browser verification via Playwright for client-side changes. The ArrowDown fix modifies inline JS in the INDEX_HTML template (a client-side behavior change), but the worker did not perform any interactive browser checks. The handoff contains no interactiveChecks entries. The fix is correct by code review (it mirrors the focus handler exactly), but the skill procedure was not fully followed for this item.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows only npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test were run. No playwright_browser_snapshot or playwright_browser_click calls appear. Skill SKILL.md step 4 says: 'Then manually verify via playwright browser tools: Navigate to http://localhost:3002/, Take a snapshot to verify UI renders correctly, Test the specific user interactions this feature adds.' Handoff skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true but no interactiveChecks are present.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The gas-price cache constants CACHE_TTL_MS and MAX_CACHE_SIZE are exported for testing but are hardcoded values. If these need tuning in different environments, they could be feature flags or environment variables. Currently this is fine for the project's scale, but if more caches with TTL/eviction are added, a shared cache utility pattern may be worth documenting.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/gas-price.ts lines 24-27: export const CACHE_TTL_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000; export const MAX_CACHE_SIZE = 1000; The project already has src/feature-flags.ts for environment-based configuration.

summary: All five polish items are implemented correctly and tested. The gas-price cache now has TTL (5min) and max-size (1000) eviction with proper logger.debug calls replacing silent error swallowing. The ArrowDown-when-closed behavior correctly mirrors the focus handler. Curve tests use distinct mock instances proving correct chain routing. The autocomplete regression test guards against using raw input value instead of getCurrentChainId(). One typo in a test name and a minor observation about eviction timing are non-blocking. The worker did not perform interactive browser verification for the ArrowDown client-side fix, but the code change is demonstrably correct by comparison with the focus handler.
